reviewer: B — Adversarial AP Student Reviewer (pilot self-audit)
scope: Student clarity, pacing, and likely misconceptions

student_confusion_checks.risk: Student thinks individual moths adapt during their lifetime.
student_confusion_checks.evidence: Sections 02 and 13 say individuals do not transform/evolve; the population changes.

student_confusion_checks.risk: Student starts Hardy-Weinberg from the dominant phenotype because the question asks for A.
student_confusion_checks.evidence: Sections 08 and 09 repeatedly tell the student to start from q^2/recessive phenotype.

student_confusion_checks.risk: Student equates fitness with strength or lifespan.
student_confusion_checks.evidence: Section 06 uses a high-grandchildren example and explicitly rejects strong/fast/impressive.
Section 11 contrasts biased reproductive success with random sampling.

pacing_notes: All sections are short enough for one visual idea per slide.
Pause arrives around the middle of the lesson and has a separate worked-answer slide.
The lesson ends with concrete Learn Portal tasks rather than a vague encouragement.
minor_notes: Could add one second pause before showing section 09 answer in final video timing.
Future version could include a second mini-check after drift vs selection.
overall_assessment: This is much easier for a student to follow than the original dense lecture. Pass for a V2 pilot.
